---
title: "Bondone_script"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-25"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
install.packages('tidyverse')
library(tidyverse)
install.packages('phyloseq') #not working

library(readr)

install.packages('iCAMP')
library(iCAMP)

library(vegan)
library(ade4)

install.packages('ecodist')
library(ecodist)

install.packages('gtools')
library(gtools)

```

```{r}
theme_set(theme_bw())
```


#install phyloseq
```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("phyloseq")
library(phyloseq)

```

```{r}
library(Biostrings)
```

####################
# *Import the files*

# import tree
```{r}
Bact_tree<-read_tree('16S/16s_bacterialtree.nwk')
```
# import fasta (this actually is not needed by phyloseq)
```{r}
Bact_fasta<-readDNAStringSet('16S/16s_bacteria_only.fasta')
```

# import OTU
```{r}
Bact_otu<-read.csv('16S/otu_table_bacteria.csv')

rownames(Bact_otu)<-Bact_otu$SeqName # assign rownames
Bact_otu$X<-NULL
Bact_otu$SeqName<-NULL

# remove the 'X' in front of sample names
colnames(Bact_otu)<- gsub( 'X', '', colnames(Bact_otu) )
```

#import sample tab
```{r}
sample_tab<-read.csv('16S/sample_table.csv')

rownames(sample_tab)<-sample_tab$uniqueID # assign rownames
sample_tab$X<-NULL
```

#import tax table
```{r}
Bact_tax<-read.csv('16S/tax_table_bacteria.csv')

rownames(Bact_tax)<-Bact_tax$SeqName # assign row names 
Bact_tax<-Bact_tax[,-c(1,2)]

```

# *import all into phyloseq*

```{r}
class(Bact_tax)
Bact_tax<-as.matrix(Bact_tax) # convert to matrix
```

# create the needed dataframes for phyloseq
```{r}
b.OTU<-otu_table(Bact_otu, taxa_are_rows = T)
b.TAX<-tax_table(Bact_tax)
b.sample<-sample_data(sample_tab)

```

# create the phyloseq file *b_ps* - all OTUs for now (will subset later)
```{r}
b_ps<-phyloseq(b.OTU, b.TAX, b.sample, Bact_tree) # this includes all otus

```

# *examine the OTU tab - check abundance, occurrences, etc*
# plot the abundance and occurrence (richness) profile of taxa per sample
*discuss if rarefying to 12k reads is needed* - 
```{r}
sort.abb<-sort(colSums(Bact_otu), decreasing = T) # sort tot abundance per sample
plot(sort.abb, type='l')

sort.occ<-sort(colSums(vegan::decostand(Bact_otu, 'pa')), decreasing = T) # sort tot richness per sample
plot(sort.occ, type='l')


# many taxa found in only 1 sample
sort_taxa_occur<-sort(rowSums(vegan::decostand(Bact_otu, 'pa'))) # sort tot occurrence per taxa (in how many samples does the taxa occur)
plot(sort_taxa_occur, type='l')

sum(sort_taxa_occur>1) # many with low occurrence


sort_taxa_abb<-sort(rowSums(Bact_otu)) # sort tot abundance per taxa
plot(sort_taxa_abb, type='l')

sum(sort_taxa_abb>5)


```


# Working on the *niche differences between taxa*
```{r}
#traspose the Otu tab
Bact_otu_t<-t(Bact_otu)
Bact_otu_t<-as.data.frame(Bact_otu_t)
```

# niche difference between OTUs (all OTUs now) bases on elevation (needed for Mantel test, etc)
```{r}
niche.dif=iCAMP::dniche(env = sample_tab[,c(2,4,5,6)], 
                        comm =Bact_otu_t,
                        method = "niche.value",
                        nworker = 4,out.dist=T, bigmemo=F,
                        nd.wd="~/Documents/FEM_work/PNRR/Bondone")
```

# check the mean and variance of each taxa over the elevation gradient- elevetion niche (using all taxa)
```{r}
elev_niche=
ade4::sco.distri(sample_tab[,2],
           Bact_otu_t ,
            labels = NULL)
```

```{r}
class(Bact_tax)
Bact_tax<-as.data.frame(Bact_tax)

```

# add phylum and Otu info on the elev_niche df
```{r}
elev_niche$Phylum= Bact_tax$Phylum[match(row.names(elev_niche), row.names(Bact_tax))]
elev_niche$OTU= rownames(elev_niche)


```


# plot elevation niche using all taxa
```{r fig.width=6, fig.height=9}
 ggplot(data=elev_niche ,
        aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))))+xlab('Elevation')+ylab('Bacterial taxa')+
    geom_point(data=elev_niche %>% select(mean, var, OTU), aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))),   size=1, shape=21, col='grey60')+geom_errorbarh(size=0.3, col='grey60')+theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0))+xlim(700, 1650)+

theme_classic()+
  
    geom_point(aes(col=Phylum ))+
 geom_errorbarh(aes(col=Phylum), size=0.3)+theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())+facet_wrap(~Phylum)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0), axis.title = element_text(size=15))+theme(legend.position = 'none')
```

#subsetting
# *subset otu table excluding taxa present in only 3 sample*

```{r}
Bact_otu_subs<-
Bact_otu %>% 
  filter(rownames(Bact_otu) %in% names(sort_taxa_occur[sort_taxa_occur>3]) ) # filter using names from the df 'sort_taxa_occur'

# these the names of taxa occurring in >3 sample
names(sort_taxa_occur[sort_taxa_occur>3])

# how many reads per OTU in the subsetted group?
sort_taxa_abb_subs<-sort(rowSums(Bact_otu_subs), decreasing = T) # within the taxa occurring in >3 samples, the min read (abb) is 5

```
# Traspose subsetted OTU tab
```{r}
Bact_otu_subs_t<-as.data.frame(t(Bact_otu_subs))
```




# calculate niche elevation, using the subset of taxa
```{r}
elev_niche_subs=
sco.distri(sample_tab[,2],
           Bact_otu_subs_t ,
            labels = NULL)
```
# add tax information
```{r}
elev_niche_subs$Phylum= Bact_tax$Phylum[match(row.names(elev_niche_subs), row.names(Bact_tax))]
elev_niche_subs$OTU= rownames(elev_niche_subs)
```

# plot elevation niche with subset of taxa (c 11k otus)
```{r fig.width=6.5, fig.height=9}
 ggplot(data=elev_niche_subs ,
        aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))))+xlab('Elevation')+ylab('Bacterial taxa')+
    geom_point(data=elev_niche_subs %>% select(mean, var, OTU), aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))),   size=1, shape=21, col='grey60')+geom_errorbarh(size=0.3, col='grey60')+theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0))+xlim(700, 1650)+

theme_classic()+
  
    geom_point(aes(col=Phylum ))+
 geom_errorbarh(aes(col=Phylum), size=0.3)+theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())+facet_wrap(~Phylum)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0), axis.title = element_text(size=15), strip.text = element_text(size=7))+theme(legend.position = 'none')
```



# *calculate niche pH*, using the subset of taxa
```{r}
pH_niche_subs=
sco.distri(sample_tab[,4],
           Bact_otu_subs_t ,
            labels = NULL)
```
# add tax information
```{r}
pH_niche_subs$Phylum= Bact_tax$Phylum[match(row.names(pH_niche_subs), row.names(Bact_tax))]
pH_niche_subs$OTU= rownames(pH_niche_subs)
```

# plot elevation niche with subset of taxa (c 11k otus)
```{r fig.width=6.5, fig.height=9}
 ggplot(data=pH_niche_subs ,
        aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))))+xlab('pH')+ylab('Bacterial taxa')+
    geom_point(data=pH_niche_subs %>% select(mean, var, OTU), aes(x=mean, y=reorder(OTU, mean), xmin=(mean-sqrt(var)), xmax=(mean+sqrt(var))),   size=1, shape=21, col='grey60')+geom_errorbarh(size=0.3, col='grey60')+theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0))+xlim(4, 8)+

theme_classic()+
  
    geom_point(aes(col=Phylum ))+
 geom_errorbarh(aes(col=Phylum), size=0.3)+theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())+facet_wrap(~Phylum)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=0, hjust=0), axis.title = element_text(size=15), strip.text = element_text(size=7))+theme(legend.position = 'none')
```







##############################################
## *Update phyloseq objet, using subset of taxa*

```{r}
b_ps_subs<-prune_taxa(taxa_names(b_ps) %in% names(Bact_otu_subs_t), b_ps )
```

# calculate the *niche difference* using subsetted phyloseq (based on 6631 otu)
```{r}
niche.dif_subs=iCAMP::dniche(env = sample_tab[,c(2,4,5,6)], 
                        comm =Bact_otu_subs_t,
                        method = "niche.value",
                        nworker = 4,out.dist=T, bigmemo=F,
                        nd.wd="~/Documents/FEM_work/PNRR/Bondone")
```

# extract the phylogen distance *pd.subs*
```{r}
pd.subs=cophenetic(phy_tree(b_ps_subs))
```

# Plot the mantel correlogram for altitude
*this takes about 10m*
```{r}
mantest.elev=
mantel.correlog(as.dist(pd.subs),as.dist(niche.dif_subs$nd$altitude), nperm = 150 )
```

```{r}
plot(mantest.elev, alpha=.05) 
title('Correlogram Elevation')
```

# Chunks of code to plot the pairwise phylo and niche differences - 
# transform the elevation and phylo distance into 3cols matrices
```{r}
elev_dist<-as.dist(niche.dif_subs$nd$altitude)
pd_dist<-as.dist(pd.subs)

elev_dist_3col<-
dist.3col(elev_dist)

elev_d

pd_dist_3col<-
  dist.3col(pd_dist)



```

# add the otu-pairs to each df
```{r}
pd_dist_3col$pairs<-paste(pd_dist_3col$name1, pd_dist_3col$name2, sep='_')

elev_dist_3col$pairs<-paste(elev_dist_3col$name1, elev_dist_3col$name2, sep='_')

```

# add the phylo distance into the elevation distance df (using the same otu pairs to guide the match)
```{r}
elev_dist_3col$pd_dist<-pd_dist_3col$dis[match(elev_dist_3col$pairs, pd_dist_3col$pairs )]

```

# explore relation between raw distances (elev vs phylo)
*strong relation for short-medium phylo distances.*
```{r}
elev_dist_3col %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(pd_dist, dis)+geom_smooth()
```



################################
# Further subset taxa (3000) towards the null models

```{r}
sort_taxa_abb_subs # this is the sorting of subsetted otu, based on total abb
names(sort_taxa_abb_subs[1:100]) # names of the first 100 otus

```
# *subset the 3000 most abbund OTUs*
```{r}
Bact_otu_3k<-
  Bact_otu_subs %>% 
  filter(rownames(Bact_otu_subs) %in% names(sort_taxa_abb_subs[1:3000]))
```
# create the transpose version of the 3k otu df
```{r}
Bact_otu_3k_t<-as.data.frame(t(Bact_otu_3k))
```

# Create the Phyloseq file with the 3k otus
*b_ps_3k*

```{r}
b_ps_3k<-prune_taxa(taxa_names(b_ps_subs) %in% names(Bact_otu_3k_t), b_ps_subs )
b_ps_3k
```
# extract the phylo tree from the 3k otu phyloseq
```{r}
pd_3k<-cophenetic(phy_tree(b_ps_3k))
```

# *First test of null model (qpen)*
10 randomization takes 3 minutes
120 randomiz in 11 minuts
```{r}
b_qp_3k=qpen(comm= Bact_otu_3k_t, pd=pd_3k, 
            tree=phy_tree(b_ps_3k),
             rand.time=500, nworker=4)
```

# see results
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result
```
# add the info on site pairs (using both pair combinations; likely not needed)
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$pair<-paste(b_qp_3k$result$sample1, b_qp_3k$result$sample2, sep=".")
b_qp_3k$result$pair2<-paste(b_qp_3k$result$sample2, b_qp_3k$result$sample1, sep=".")

```


# calculate *elevation distance between samples*
```{r}
elev_dist_samples<-
sample_tab %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(altitude, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')
```
# transform elevation distance into 3col matrix
```{r}
elev_dist_samples.df<-dist.3col(elev_dist_samples)
elev_dist_samples.df$pairs<-paste(elev_dist_samples.df$name1, elev_dist_samples.df$name2, sep=".")
elev_dist_samples.df$pairs2<-paste(elev_dist_samples.df$name2, elev_dist_samples.df$name1, sep=".") # maybe not needed

```

# calculate *pH distance between samples*
```{r}
pH_dist_samples<-
sample_tab %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(pH, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')
```
# transform pH distance into 3col matrix
```{r}
pH_dist_samples.df<-dist.3col(pH_dist_samples)
pH_dist_samples.df$pairs<-paste(pH_dist_samples.df$name1, pH_dist_samples.df$name2, sep=".")
```



# add the elevation distance to the null model results
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$elev_dist<-elev_dist_samples.df$dis[match(b_qp_3k$result$pair, elev_dist_samples.df$pairs)]
sum(is.na(b_qp_3k$result$elev_dist))
```

# work on a temporary df (from the sample pairs only) where I split the sample pairs into additional pairing information
```{r}
tmp<-b_qp_3k$result[,c(1,2)] # only use the sample pairs info

#split the sample 1 (of the pair) into replicate, horizon, and A,B info
tmp<- 
tmp %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(sample1, delim = '_', names=c('S1_repl', 'S1_horiz', "S1_AB"), too_few = 'align_start')

#do as above for sample 2 (of the pair)
tmp<-
tmp %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(sample2, delim = '_', names=c('S2_repl', 'S2_horiz', "S2_AB"), too_few = 'align_start')
```

# add horizon pair match on the tmp data
```{r}
# if same horizon, or different horizon
tmp$horiz_pair<-
ifelse(tmp$S1_horiz == tmp$S2_horiz, 'Same_horiz', 'diff_horiz')

# add horizon type info (OR, MIN) for the same horizon pairs, otherwise write 'Mix horizon' (when not the same horizon between the pair of samples)
tmp$horiz_type<-
  ifelse(tmp$horiz_pair=='Same_horiz', tmp$S1_horiz, 'Mix_horiz')
```

# add the sample pairing information into the null model results
*must rerun the null model. I have cancelled part of the results by doing this!*
```{r}
# use b_qp_3k$results!!
b_qp_3k$result<-
cbind(b_qp_3k$result, tmp)

```

*add the pH, but need to redo it properly once I run the null again*
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$pH_dist<-pH_dist_samples.df$dis[match(b_qp_3k$result$pair, pH_dist_samples.df$pairs)]
```



```{r}
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  filter(horiz_type != 'Mix_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(elev_dist, bNTI, col=horiz_type)+geom_smooth(method='lm')+geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')+
  geom_point()

b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  filter(horiz_type != 'Mix_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(elev_dist, bNTI, col=horiz_type)+geom_smooth(method='lm')+geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')
```


```{r}
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  filter(horiz_type != 'Mix_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(pH_dist, bNTI, col=horiz_type)+geom_smooth(method='lm')+geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')+
  xlab('pH distance between samples')
```




# calculat just bNTI, as distamce (to be used in MRM)
*combining all samples*
```{r}
b.bNTI.dist<-
  bNTIn.p(comm=Bact_otu_3k_t, dis=pd_3k,rand=400, 
          nworker=4, output.bMNTD = T)
```

# Multiple reg on distance matrices

```{r}
library(ecodist)
install.packages('MDMR')
library(MDMR)
```

# *multiuple distance matrix regression indicates that pH is the key driver of bNTI variation*
# here combining all samples
```{r}
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(b.bNTI.dist$index)) ~ as.vector(elev_dist)) # R2=0.027; p=0.001
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(b.bNTI.dist$index)) ~ as.vector(pH_dist_samples)) # R2=0.557; p=0.001

MRM(as.vector(as.dist(b.bNTI.dist$index)) ~ as.vector(elev_dist)+ as.vector(pH_dist_samples)) # R2=0.559

```

# extract sample tab *covariates* for min and or layers
```{r}
sample_tab_MIN<-
sample_tab %>% 
  filter(horizon == 'MIN_A' | horizon =='MIN_B') 

sample_tab_OR<-
sample_tab %>% 
  filter(horizon == 'OR') 

```

# extract the pH and elevation distance between samples, for MIN and OR separate
```{r}

#MIN
pH_dist_samples_MIN<-
sample_tab_MIN %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(pH, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')


elev_dist_samples_MIN<-
sample_tab_MIN %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(altitude, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')


# OR
pH_dist_samples_OR<-
sample_tab_OR %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(pH, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')


elev_dist_samples_OR<-
sample_tab_OR %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(altitude, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')

```


# Subset the OTU 3k (trasposed) for each horizon (layer)
```{r}
Bact_otu_3k_t_MIN<-
  Bact_otu_3k_t %>% 
  filter(rownames(Bact_otu_3k_t) %in% rownames(sample_tab_MIN))
           
           
           
Bact_otu_3k_t_OR<-
  Bact_otu_3k_t %>% 
  filter(rownames(Bact_otu_3k_t) %in% rownames(sample_tab_OR))


```


# *Subset phyloseq for OR and MIN separately*
To extract e.g. phylo distance for separate samples/taxa combination
```{r}
#phyloseq just for MIN
b_ps_3k_MIN<-prune_samples(rownames(Bact_otu_3k_t_MIN), b_ps_3k) # subset samples
b_ps_3k_MIN<- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(b_ps_3k_MIN)>0, b_ps_3k_MIN) # remove taxa that now have 0 abund


#and for OR
b_ps_3k_OR<-prune_samples(rownames(Bact_otu_3k_t_OR), b_ps_3k) # subset samples
b_ps_3k_OR<- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(b_ps_3k_OR)>0, b_ps_3k_OR) # remove taxa that now have 0 abund
```
# extract the phylo distance for MIN and OR

```{r}
pd_3k_MIN<-cophenetic(phy_tree(b_ps_3k_MIN))

pd_3k_OR<-cophenetic(phy_tree(b_ps_3k_OR))

```

# Calculate bNTI for MIN and OR separately
#bNTI for MIN
```{r}
b.bNTI.dist_MIN<-
  bNTIn.p(comm=Bact_otu_3k_t_MIN, dis=pd_3k_MIN,rand=400, 
          nworker=4, output.bMNTD = T)
```
#bNTI for OR
```{r}
b.bNTI.dist_OR<-
  bNTIn.p(comm=Bact_otu_3k_t_OR, dis=pd_3k_OR,rand=400, 
          nworker=4, output.bMNTD = T)
```


# Run MRM (Multiple regression matrices) on MIN and OR separately
#To check which driver is more influential towards bNTI for MIN and OR

*we see again the pH is the main driver underpinning bNTI variation*
*and that pH has stronger effect on MIN layers, rather than OR*
```{r}
#MRM on MIN
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(elev_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta_elev=0.22; R2=0.044; p=0.001
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta_pH=0.8; R2=0.59; p=0.001

MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(elev_dist_samples_MIN))+ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta_elev=0.006; beta_ph=0.80; R2=0.59; negligible effect of elevation 

# MRM on OR
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(elev_dist_samples_OR))) # beta_elev=0.11;  R2=0.14, p=0.019
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_OR))) # beta_pH=0.46; R2 = 0.21

MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(elev_dist_samples_OR))+ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_OR))) # beta_ph=0.47; beta_elev=-0.03; R2=0.21
```




####### Plots of *processes* contribution over elevation / pH distances group #########
################################################################################
# create the quantile cut for elevation distance and pH distance on the null model results


```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$elev_dist_q<-
  quantcut(b_qp_3k$result$elev_dist, 3)


b_qp_3k$result$pH_dist_q<-
  quantcut(b_qp_3k$result$pH_dist,3)
```

# mutate heterogen to variable in assembly processes
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result<-
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
 mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable"))

unique(b_qp_3k$result$process)
```


# Plot of processes over three quantiles of elevation diff (all layers combined)
```{r}

p1.elev<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
 filter(elev_dist_q =="[0,117]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0-117 m')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p2.elev<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
 filter(elev_dist_q =="(117,316]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('117--316 m')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p3.elev<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
 filter(elev_dist_q =="(316,639]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('316-639 m')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


```


```{r}
install.packages('ggpubr')
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
```

# the combined plot of processes over elevation diff
*for all samples across layers*
```{r}
process_elev_plot_ALL<-
ggarrange(p1.elev, p2.elev, p3.elev,  ncol=3, nrow=1, common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(process_elev_plot_ALL, top=text_grob('All layers          ', size=14) ,
                bottom = textGrob("Pairwise elevation difference                   ", gp = gpar(cex = 1.3)))
```

# Plot of processes over pH differences (combingin all layers)
```{r}
p1.pH<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
 filter(pH_dist_q =="[0,0.51]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0 - 0.51 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p2.pH<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
     filter(pH_dist_q =="(0.51,1.45]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0.51 - 1.45 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p3.pH<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
 filter(pH_dist_q =="(1.45,2.89]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('1.45 - 2.89 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))
```

# combined plot of processes for min layer over pH
#*clear deterministic selection over the pH gradient*

```{r}
process_pH_plot_ALL<-
ggarrange(p1.pH, p2.pH, p3.pH,  ncol=3, nrow=1, common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(process_pH_plot_ALL, top=text_grob('All layers          ', size=14) ,
                bottom = textGrob("Pairwise pH difference                   ", gp = gpar(cex = 1.3)))
```






##############################################################
# Plots of processes across pH quantiles, separated for layers

# MIN layers processes plot
```{r}
p1.pH_MIN<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
  filter(horiz_type=='MIN') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="[0,0.51]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0 - 0.51 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p2.pH_MIN<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
    filter(horiz_type=='MIN') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="(0.51,1.45]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0.51 - 1.45 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p3.pH_MIN<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
    filter(horiz_type=='MIN') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="(1.45,2.89]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('1.45 - 2.89 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))
```

# combined plot of processes for min layer over pH
```{r}
process_pH_plot_MIN<-
ggarrange(p1.pH_MIN, p2.pH_MIN, p3.pH_MIN,  ncol=3, nrow=1, common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(process_pH_plot_MIN, top=text_grob('Mineral layer          ', size=14) ,
                bottom = textGrob("Pairwise pH difference                   ", gp = gpar(cex = 1.3)))
```





# ORlayers processes
```{r}
p1.pH_OR<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
  filter(horiz_type=='OR') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="[0,0.51]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0 - 0.51 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p2.pH_OR<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
    filter(horiz_type=='OR') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="(0.51,1.45]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('0.51 - 1.45 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))


p3.pH_OR<-
b_qp_3k$result %>%  
    filter(horiz_type=='OR') %>% 
 filter(pH_dist_q =="(1.45,2.89]") %>% 
  mutate(process=as.factor(process)) %>% 
  #mutate(process= str_replace(process, "Heterogeneous", "Variable")) %>% 
  mutate(process=factor(process, levels=c('Dispersal.Limitation', 'Variable.Selection','Homogeneous.Selection' , 'Homogenizing.Dispersal', 'Undominated'))) %>%
  count(process) %>% 
  mutate(pct=n/sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot+ aes(x='',y=pct, fill=process)+ geom_bar(stat='identity') +xlab('1.45 - 2.89 pH')+ylab(NULL)+theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("seagreen", "orange", "blueviolet", "firebrick3", "grey70"))
```


# the plot of processes for Organic layer
*need to think how to interpret increasing disp. limitation over the pH gradient, just for OR layer*
# *Eventually speak with James*
```{r}
process_pH_plot_OR<-
ggarrange(p1.pH_OR, p2.pH_OR, p3.pH_OR,  ncol=3, nrow=1, common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(process_pH_plot_OR, top=text_grob('Organic layer          ', size=14) ,
                bottom = textGrob("Pairwise pH difference                   ", gp = gpar(cex = 1.3)))
```



##############
# Add more pairing information, indicating the samples pairs coming from the same elevation step. 


```{r}

# this just takes the sample number and remove the 5 replicates, creating new column
b_qp_3k$result<-
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  mutate( S1 = str_sub(S1_repl, 1,1))

b_qp_3k$result<-
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  mutate( S2 = str_sub(S2_repl, 1,1))

# indicate of samples belong to same or differe 'S' (sample location = elevation)
b_qp_3k$result$S_match<-
  ifelse(b_qp_3k$result$S1 == b_qp_3k$result$S2, 'Same_S', 'Diff_S')

b_qp_3k$result$S_num<-
  ifelse(b_qp_3k$result$S_match =='Same_S', b_qp_3k$result$S1, 'Mix_S')
```

# add Sample number (w/o replicate N, S, C, etc) to the sample table
this is needed to add actual elevation info inot the null model results (only for the samples coming from same elevation)

```{r}
sample_tab<-
sample_tab %>% 
  mutate(S_num = str_sub(uniqueID, 1, 1))
```

# add actual elevation and other drivers to the null model res
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$elevation<-
  sample_tab$altitude[match(b_qp_3k$result$S_num, sample_tab$S_num)]

b_qp_3k$result$pH<-
  sample_tab$pH[match(b_qp_3k$result$S_num, sample_tab$S_num)]


```


# No apparent homogenization (bray curtis) among replicates across the elevation (or pH) gradient
# however there is a sign of *weaker homogenising selection as pH increase (lower pH values act as filter)*
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result %>%
  filter(S_match=='Same_S') %>% 
  filter(horiz_pair=='Same_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(y=BC, x=as.factor(elevation), col= horiz_type)+geom_boxplot()+
  ylab('BrayCurtis between replicates')

b_qp_3k$result %>%
  filter(S_match=='Same_S') %>% 
  filter(horiz_pair=='Same_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(y=BC, x=as.factor(pH), col= horiz_type)+geom_boxplot()+
  ylab('BrayCurtis between replicates')

b_qp_3k$result %>%
  filter(S_match=='Same_S') %>% 
  filter(horiz_pair=='Same_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(y=bNTI, x=pH, col= horiz_type)+
  #geom_smooth(aes(y=bNTI, x=pH))+
  geom_boxplot(aes(y=bNTI, x=as.factor(pH))) +
  ylab('BrayCurtis between replicates')+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')
  

```
# Boxplot and trends for within sample bNTI vs pH values
# highlights prevalent homogenising selection with low pH values (acidic soil)
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result %>%
  filter(S_match == 'Same_S', horiz_pair == 'Same_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = pH, y = bNTI, color = horiz_type, fill = horiz_type)) +
  # 1. Add the boxplots first (keeping x as numeric)
  geom_boxplot(aes(group = interaction(pH, horiz_type)), 
               alpha = 0.3, 
               outlier.shape = NA) + 
  # 2. Add the smooth line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  # 3. Aesthetics
  labs(y = "bNTI between replicates", 
       x = "Soil pH") +
  theme_bw()+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')
```

```{r}
b_qp_3k$result
```




# Calculate distances in cabon and nitrogen content among sites (all samples)

```{r}
TN_dist_samples<-
sample_tab %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(TN, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')
```

# TN distance for MIN layer
```{r}
TN_dist_samples_MIN<-
sample_tab_MIN %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(TN, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')
```

# TN distance for OR layer
```{r}
TN_dist_samples_OR<-
sample_tab_OR %>% 
  mutate(dummy=1) %>% 
  select(TN, dummy) %>% 
vegdist( 'euclidean')
```




# convert Nitrogen dist to 3column (all samples)
```{r}
TN_dist_3col <-
  dist.3col(TN_dist_samples)

TN_dist_3col$pair<-paste(TN_dist_3col$name1, TN_dist_3col$name2, sep=".")
```

# add the TN distance to the null model res
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result$TN_dist<-
  TN_dist_3col$dis[match(b_qp_3k$result$pair, TN_dist_3col$pair)]
```

# plot bNTI vs TN distance
*mirrors the results from pH*
```{r}
b_qp_3k$result %>% 
  filter(horiz_type != 'Mix_horiz') %>% 
  ggplot()+aes(TN_dist, bNTI, col=horiz_type)+geom_smooth(method='lm')+geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2,2), col='black', linetype='dashed')+
  xlab('TN distance between samples')
```


# MRM on TN distance and pH dist
*using scaled distances*
```{r}
#MRM on MIN
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(TN_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta=0.63 p=0.001; r2=0.36
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta=0.8; R2=0.589; p=0.001

MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_MIN$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(TN_dist_samples_MIN))+ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_MIN))) # beta_ph=0.65; beta_TN=0.31; R2=0.66; 

# MRM on OR
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(TN_dist_samples_OR))) # beta_TN=0.39; R2=0.15, p=0.001
MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_OR))) # Beta_pH=0.46 R2=0.2 

MRM(as.vector(as.dist(scale(b.bNTI.dist_OR$index))) ~ scale(as.vector(TN_dist_samples_OR))+ scale(as.vector(pH_dist_samples_OR))) # beta_TN=0.31; beta_pH=0.40;  R2=0.3
```
